summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates filesystem listing to attacker-controlled OAST endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that executes automatically during `npm install`. The script runs `curl -X POST` with a recursive directory listing of `/data/` as the request body, targeting `http://3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com/data` — a Burp Collaborator (oastify.com) out-of-band exfiltration subdomain. The package advertises itself as a generic string utility but ships no functional code matching that purpose; metadata fields (author, repository, homepage, bugs) are empty placeholders, consistent with a disposable throwaway package. Installing this package causes the installer's filesystem contents to be transmitted in cleartext to an attacker-controlled host with no legitimate purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
